intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated transitions between dashboard views over instant loads, creating a sense of continuity and reducing cognitive jarring
facade.label: Smooth slide in
facade.acceptImplies: User values fluid motion and visual continuity; likely prefers eased transitions, parallax effects, and animated state changes throughout the dashboard
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes snappy responsiveness and instant feedback; likely prefers crisp, click-based navigation with minimal motion overhead

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer animated transitions between dashboard views over instant loads, creating a sense of continuity and reducing cognitive jarring
evidence.implication: User values fluid motion and visual continuity; likely prefers eased transitions, parallax effects, and animated state changes throughout the dashboard

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer seeing one key metric prominently displayed rather than narrative context for health data
facade.label: Big number, small story
facade.acceptImplies: User wants dashboard hierarchy centered on metrics-first design—prominent KPIs, de-emphasized explanatory text
facade.rejectImplies: User wants balanced or narrative-forward design—context and story matter as much as the numbers themselves

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer calming, low-contrast interfaces that reduce visual fatigue during frequent check-ins
facade.label: Soft shadows, muted colors
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use gentle gradients, desaturated palette, and diffused lighting to create a restful monitoring environment
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer crisp contrast, vibrant accents, or high-definition clarity for data visibility and engagement

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments need visual calm and spaciousness—not dense dashboards that create anxiety—to stay engaged long-term
facade.label: Minimal data, maximum breathing room
facade.acceptImplies: User values uncluttered, peaceful interfaces that reduce cognitive load and foster reflection over constant monitoring
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers information density, compact layouts, or visual richness to feel in control and see patterns at a glance

facade.hypothesis: Users want motivational, celebratory tone—not clinical detachment—when reviewing health experiment results
facade.label: Encouraging coach voice
facade.acceptImplies: Users respond to warmth and celebration in health tracking. Design should use positive reinforcement language, emojis, and congratulatory messaging. Consider conversational tone in alerts and summaries.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer neutral, fact-based reporting without emotional framing. Design should be clinical, data-forward, minimal personality. Tone should be objective and restrained.

facade.hypothesis: A left sidebar navigation paired with card-based content layout will feel organized and support scanning multiple health metrics without overwhelming the dashboard.
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure: sidebar vs. top nav; information grouping: cards vs. lists
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers vertical hierarchy, compact lateral nav, and modular card-based content chunks. Good signal for: persistent navigation, metric cards, experiment grouping.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer top navigation, horizontal scanning, list views, or full-width layouts. Suggests: tabs, linear layouts, or dense information grids might work better.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer instant state changes (buttons triggering immediate data view shifts) over animated transitions when rapid metric checking is the primary task, revealing a tension between visual polish and task efficiency
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity vs task speed — do users want flow or snappy responsiveness?
facade.label: Tap to jump
facade.acceptImplies: User prioritizes quick access to specific health metrics; dashboard should support instant view switches, minimal animation overhead, fast load states. May indicate preference for gesture-based shortcuts and click-to-reveal patterns.
facade.rejectImplies: User confirms preference for animated continuity even under time pressure; willing to accept slight motion overhead for visual coherence. Suggests design should lean into eased transitions, choreographed reveals, and state-change animations throughout the interface.

draft.title: Health Experiment Tracker
draft.summary: A personal dashboard for monitoring long-term health experiments with experiment cards, metrics, and progress visualization.
draft.html:
Health Experiments

Track your long-term health initiatives

Active Experiments

Sleep Optimization

Active

Testing consistent 10 PM bedtime for 8 weeks

Week 5 of 8 62%
Avg Sleep Duration 7.2 hrs
Sleep Quality 7.8/10

Hydration Tracking

Active

Drinking 3L water daily for 12 weeks

Week 4 of 12 35%
Daily Average 2.8L
Consistency 89%
Completed Experiments

Morning Walks

Completed

30-minute walks 5 days per week for 6 weeks

Week 6 of 6 100%
